text: 𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞𝐥 𝐧𝐮𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐠𝐞 𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖 𝐭𝐨 𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖𝟖 swapped 2665 shares of $HIMS for 67291.25$ price at time of sellling: 25.25$ total in: 69963 total out: 67291.25 PnL: -2671.75$ (-3.82%) dancing on too many weddings at the same time. or how GCR said "he who chases two rabbits catches neither". 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞𝐬 𝐭𝐨 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬: i noticed a problem in my trading. lately i was more sensitive to volatility than usual. was always sensitive to pa and i think that is a strength and the reason i rarely roundtrip stuff. but it increased lately and got noticeably harder for me to stomach volatility, to a point where the negatives outweight the benefits and it is now interfering with my trading. trying to find a solution to this and think about how to change that. my first attempt of solving this is removing the TAM (total assets under managment) number and the progress bar that includes uPnL. i think constantly calculating my TAM und put an exact number to uPnL every time i open/close a trade results in me clinging to uPnL more than usual. i am internalizing this number and take it as the status quo / my NW and this results in me being overprotective. and this is bad because it reduces the ability to sit with trades and give them the necessary breathing room. and since the goal is to push the realized bar to 100% anyway i will try to ride with just one metric for a while and see how it goes and how i feel with it. 𝐡𝐨𝐥𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬: 845266 USDC 553k POD 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐛𝐚𝐫 (𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞𝐝): ▮▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯▯ 9.50%

text: Ethereum Launches Clear Signing Standard to Reduce Blind Signing Risks Ethereum Foundation said Ethereum has launched the "Clear Signing" open standard, aimed at reducing blind signing risks by displaying transaction details in human-readable text instead of unreadable hexadecimal data during transaction approvals. Contributors to the initiative include Ledger, Trezor, MetaMask, WalletConnect, and Fireblocks, with the Ethereum Foundation acting as a coordinator for the effort. — link

text: Wintermute Says BTC Rally Looks More Like a Short Squeeze Than a Healthy Breakout Wintermute said U.S. equities extended gains for a sixth straight week, with the Nasdaq and S&P 500 hitting fresh highs, while Bitcoin climbed above $80,000 for the first time since January and briefly reached around $83,000, reclaiming its 200-day moving average after months of pressure. Wintermute cautioned that the move looks more like a short squeeze than a "healthy" breakout, noting open interest rose from about $48 billion to $58 billion over the past month while spot volumes remain near two-year lows—suggesting forced short covering rather than sustained spot demand. The firm added that this week's CPI release and the Fed chair transition could be key macro catalysts. — link

text: US Senate Banking Committee Releases Crypto Clarity Act Draft Bill. > Section 404 (Prohibiting Interest and Yield on Payment Stablecoins) bans digital asset service providers from paying interest or yield purely for holding payment stablecoins, protecting bank deposits from direct competition. > However, activity-based rewards tied to usage, payments, staking, governance, or loyalty programs remain explicitly permitted. > Section 401 allows national banks, state banks, and credit unions to offer digital asset services like custody, staking, lending, payments, market making, and underwriting as activities incidental to banking, without requiring prior regulatory approval Source:

text: Fluid Says Resolv Exploit Led to $21M Bad Debt, Now Fully Covered DeFi liquidity protocol Fluid said the March Resolv exploit, which involved the malicious minting of about $80 million in uncollateralized USR, left Fluid with roughly $21 million in bad debt. Fluid said the attack was external and did not compromise its smart contracts or code. The remaining $19.3 million in bad debt has been fully covered, with Resolv covering about $9.7 million, Fluid's governance treasury covering $8.2 million, and the team covering $1.5 million. Fluid will pause FLUID buybacks, cut or remove incentives to rebuild its treasury, and plans to upgrade its oracle and pricing systems while rolling out DEX v2, Solana DEX, fixed-rate borrowing, LaaS, and custodied collateral products. — link

text: INTEL: Ray Dalio says Bitcoin has failed as a safe-haven asset, trading more like tech as investors sell it for liquidity during stress while gold remains the core reserve hedge Twitter
author: admin#2457358877

text: Ray Dalio Says Bitcoin Has Not Behaved Like a Safe-Haven Asset Bridgewater founder Ray Dalio said Bitcoin has not played the safe-haven role some investors expected, citing its lack of privacy, the potential for transactions to be monitored or restricted, and its relatively high correlation with tech stocks. Dalio argued that when other assets come under pressure, investors often sell Bitcoin to raise liquidity. He also said Bitcoin's market remains relatively small and easier to influence, while gold continues to hold a central role in the global financial system due to its broader ownership and longer-established status. — link

text: INTEL: Grayscale files to launch first-ever spot ETF tied to a privacy coin through Zcash $ZEC Trust conversion. Twitter
author: admin#2457358877

text: Grayscale Files to Launch the First Spot ETF for a Privacy Coin Grayscale has filed to convert its Zcash Trust into a spot ETF, which, if approved, would become the world's first spot ETF tied to a privacy coin. The filing comes after the U.S. SEC reportedly ended its long-running review of privacy coins without taking enforcement action, removing a key regulatory overhang. Multicoin Capital co-founder Tushar Jain also disclosed that the crypto hedge fund has been building a large ZEC position since February, viewing it as a macro hedge. — link

text: Aptos to Launch Native Encrypted Mempool to Prevent Frontrunning and Censorship Aptos said it will launch a native Encrypted Mempool to protect user transaction intent at the protocol layer, reducing risks such as frontrunning, censorship, and order-flow leakage. The mechanism keeps transaction details hidden during block ordering and decrypts them before execution, while confirmed transactions will still be recorded on-chain as usual. Aptos Labs said its batched threshold encryption design can enable the feature with minimal impact on network latency and trust assumptions. — link

text: Arthur Hayes: BTC Has Bottomed at $60K, $126K Inevitable BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes published a new essay titled The Butterfly Touch, arguing that the global AI infrastructure arms race, surging war expenditures, and nations' shift toward infrastructure buildout and critical commodity stockpiling will drive continued credit expansion in both USD and RMB — strongly bullish for Bitcoin and crypto assets. Hayes believes Bitcoin has already put in a bottom near $60,000. He sees a retest and eventual breakout above $126,000 as inevitable, with momentum likely accelerating sharply once it clears $90,000.Additionally, his family office Maelstrom is currently heavily positioned in HYPE and ZEC, and he indicated that NEAR is the next major focus for deployment. — link

text: Highlight Clip Arbitrum SC Member: My First Project Was The DAO Griff Green, a member of the Arbitrum Security Council, notes that the public often misunderstands blockchains, which actually rely on social consensus and aren't entirely immutable. Drawing from his early experience with The DAO, he explains that rules on any chain can be altered as long as the community reaches a consensus. He highlights that Arbitrum's recent use of a multi-sig mechanism to handle a crisis marks the first time a major L2 network has proactively frozen funds. — link
